articles.summary: Join Professor Hannah Fry as she interviews some of the leading voices in artificial intelligence. From recent Nobel winner, Demis Hassabis, to the legendary Jeff Dean, this series covers everything you're curious about, including the early days of Google, recent breakthroughs in GenAI, Astra, and AlphaFold, or more pressing questions like _how human should an AI assistant be?_

articles.title: COHERE'S MULTILINGUAL MODEL'S DRAMATIC IMPROVEMENT
articles.summary: One of the best efforts at multilingual language model training, the Aya project has grown substantially since its launch earlier this year. Most of the gains in performance have come from strong post training recipes. It also processes audio input and can generate images all from non-English input.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC ANALYSIS TOOL
articles.summary: Claude can now write and execute code as part of artifacts.

articles.title: GRAMMY-WINNING MUSICIAN AND GOOGLE UNVEIL MUSICFX DJ
articles.summary: Jacob Collier, a Grammy-winning musician, has teamed up with Google DeepMind and Google Labs to create MusicFX DJ, an AI-powered music tool. The interface has been redesigned to encourage creativity and help users easily enter a "flow state" of artistic inspiration. MusicFX DJ is available now, offering intuitive controls for all skill levels.

articles.title: OMNIPARSER
articles.summary: Screenshot parsing tool for models to use digital interfaces.

articles.title: META'S QUANTIZED SMALL MODELS
articles.summary: Meta has quantized its 1B and 3B language models to be 2-4x faster at minimal loss of quality while being 50%+ smaller. This was enabled by its quantization-aware training setup, which allows for models to adjust to the small precision.

articles.title: FAST POINT CLOUD UPSAMPLING
articles.summary: A simple yet efficient method for upsampling and cleaning point clouds using a modified octree-based 3D U-Net (OUNet).

articles.title: SCALE AUTOMATED WEB TESTING WITHOUT SCALING HEADCOUNT
articles.summary: QA Wolf (4.8 ⭐ on G2) [13] brings AI-level scale and efficiency to testing, with unlimited parallel test runs on their cloud infrastructure and a zero-flake guarantee.

articles.title: EXECUTORCH
articles.summary: ExecuTorch enables on-device inference on mobile and edge devices including wearables, embedded devices, and microcontrollers. It also enables efficient deployment of PyTorch models to edge devices. ExecuTorch is compatible with a wide variety of computing platforms and it can fully utilize hardware capabilities such as CPUs, NPUs, and DSPs. Step-by-step tutorials on how to use ExecuTorch are available.

articles.title: FEDERATED TRANSFORMER
articles.summary: Federated Transformer (FeT) is a new framework designed to improve the performance and privacy of Vertical Federated Learning (VFL) across multiple parties.

articles.title: A MORE EFFICIENT VISION-LANGUAGE MODEL
articles.summary: ADEM-VL is a new vision-language model designed to overcome hardware limitations in existing models.

articles.title: PREDICTING WEIGHT LOSS WITH MACHINE LEARNING
articles.summary: This author used a simple feedforward DNN model to track and predict weight loss on a ketogenic diet. The model captured the non-linear weight loss pattern, fit a function to the data, and visualized calorie metrics. The Harris-Benedict Equation was used to compare calorie needs to real weight loss for better insights.

articles.title: VIDEO SCRAPING: EXTRACTING JSON DATA FROM A 35 SECOND SCREEN CAPTURE FOR LESS THAN 1/10TH OF A CENT
articles.summary: Google Gemini's AI Studio can successfully extract numerical data from a video screen recording of emails. The process involves using the inexpensive Gemini 1.5 Flash model and incurs almost no cost. This novel "video scraping" approach offers a practical alternative to traditional data extraction methods.

articles.title: THE AI INVESTMENT BOOM
articles.summary: The AI boom is driving significant US investment in data centers, computing infrastructure, and advanced hardware, with data center construction reaching a record-high $28.6 billion annually. This surge is fueled by the growing demand for powerful computing resources necessary for training and deploying advanced AI models. While revenue rebounds in the tech sector, job growth remains concentrated in semiconductor manufacturing and infrastructure, diverting focus from traditional programming roles.

articles.title: APPLE INTERNALLY BELIEVES THAT IT'S AT LEAST TWO YEARS BEHIND IN AI DEVELOPMENT
articles.summary: Internal studies show ChatGPT is 25% more accurate than Siri, with Apple being about two years behind industry leaders in AI.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY IS REPORTEDLY LOOKING TO FUNDRAISE AT AN $8B VALUATION
articles.summary: Perplexity, an AI search engine, is in talks to raise $500 million, potentially doubling its valuation to $8 billion.

articles.title: CHINESE HUMANOID ROBOT IS THE 'FASTEST IN THE WORLD' THANKS TO ITS TRUSTY PAIR OF SNEAKERS
articles.summary: The STAR1 robot, powered by AI algorithms and high-torque motors, achieved a record speed of over 8 mph in a Gobi Desert test.
